title: Agricultural Bank of Egypt (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Agricultural Bank of Egypt in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Agricultural Bank of Egypt?
faq.answer: Agricultural Bank of Egypt (ABE) is a state-owned specialized agricultural bank and one of the largest agricultural banks in the Arab world and Middle East. The bank focuses primarily on financing and supporting Egypt's agricultural sector while also providing comprehensive banking services to rural and urban communities. It is wholly owned by the Egyptian government and operates under the supervision of the Central Bank of Egypt. With over 1,200 branches and village banks spread across Egypt, ABE serves approximately 3 to 4 million farmers and rural dwellers. The bank employs around 17,000 staff members and has established itself as a critical development institution for agricultural activity throughout the country.

faq.question: When was Agricultural Bank of Egypt founded?
faq.answer: The Agricultural Bank of Egypt was founded in 1930 with a capital of one million pounds through Royal Decree. It was originally established under the name "The Principal Bank for Development and Agricultural Credit" during the global economic crisis of 1931. The bank's original purpose was to provide loans to Egyptian farmers and protect them from foreign real estate banks and moneylenders who charged high interest rates. This mission was crucial during a period when Egypt's economy relied heavily on agriculture, which employed a significant portion of the population. In 2016, pursuant to Law No. 84 of 2016, the bank was transformed into a public sector bank and renamed "Agricultural Bank of Egypt" (ABE). The law stipulated that the bank would take the form of an Egyptian joint stock company with its capital entirely owned by the state, giving it an independent legal personality while maintaining its developmental role in Egypt's agricultural sector.

faq.question: Where is Agricultural Bank of Egypt headquartered?
faq.answer: Agricultural Bank of Egypt is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt. The bank's administrative head office is located at 110 Al Kasr Al Aini Street in the Qasr El Nil district of Cairo. From this central location, the bank coordinates its extensive operations across the country through its network of district branches, village banks, and specialized service centers.

faq.question: Where does Agricultural Bank of Egypt operate?
faq.answer: Agricultural Bank of Egypt operates throughout all governorates of Egypt, with a particular focus on rural areas and villages. The bank has established over 1,200 branches and village banks that cover the entire Egyptian countryside. The bank has significant presence in governorates including Sharqeya, Gharbeya, Beheira, Port Said, Assiut, Sohag, Fayoum, Ismailia, Beni Suef, Damietta, Dakahleya, Menia, Giza, Kafr El Sheikh, Kaliobeya, Qena, and Alexandria. The extensive branch network ensures that financial services reach farmers and rural communities in even the most remote areas of the country. ABE has been expanding its reach with plans to increase the number of branches to 2,000 and grow its customer base to 30-35 million customers across Egypt within the next few years.

faq.question: What services does Agricultural Bank of Egypt offer?
faq.answer: Agricultural Bank of Egypt provides a comprehensive range of banking and financial services tailored to both agricultural and general banking needs: Agricultural Financing: The bank offers specialized agricultural loans for purchasing seeds, fertilizers, pesticides, equipment, and livestock. It also provides financing for land development, irrigation systems, modern agricultural machinery, and renewable energy projects in agriculture. The bank's loan portfolio reached EGP 80.3 billion by September 2024. Microfinance: ABE operates microfinance programs offering small loans ranging from EGP 2,000 to EGP 10,000 for small-scale farmers, rural entrepreneurs, handicrafts, poultry and livestock raising, and household industries. Commercial Banking: The bank provides traditional banking services including current and savings accounts, deposit accounts, time deposits, certificates of deposit, and various payment services. The deposit portfolio reached EGP 189.4 billion by September 2024. Digital Services: ABE has undergone significant digital transformation with the launch of the Agri-Wallet mobile wallet application, InstaPay integration for instant transfers, mobile and internet banking platforms, and the FINICAL banking system (launched in recent years as the latest core banking technology). The bank also issues Farmer Cards - specialized payment cards for agricultural financing that restrict expenditures to agricultural merchants and services. Storage Services: The bank operates 392 storage facilities (shouns) with over 4 million square meters of storage capacity, with 2 million square meters specifically allocated for receiving local wheat from farmers.

faq.question: What makes Agricultural Bank of Egypt unique?
faq.answer: Agricultural Bank of Egypt stands out as the largest specialized agricultural bank in the Arab world and Middle East, with several distinctive features: Specialized Agricultural Focus: Unlike general commercial banks, ABE has developed specialized underwriting systems specifically designed for agricultural loans, understanding the unique risks and cycles of farming activities. The bank works closely with the Ministry of Agriculture and Land Reclamation on various agricultural development initiatives. Farmer Card Innovation: ABE pioneered the Farmer Card payment system in Egypt, which creates a controlled mechanism for agricultural credit by restricting farmers' expenditures to authorized agricultural merchants and services. Over 685,000 Farmer Cards have been distributed across multiple governorates, creating a value chain that connects farmers with farming products, trade, and industry. Rural Banking Infrastructure: The bank operates the most extensive rural banking network in Egypt, with branches in villages and rural areas where other banks typically do not operate. This includes operating village banks that serve as the primary financial institution for many rural communities. Digital Transformation for Agriculture: ABE launched the Agri-Wallet, Egypt's first mobile wallet specifically designed for the agricultural sector. This provides financial services to the informal sector in rural areas, many of whom previously had no access to formal banking. The wallet enables bill payments, government service payments through the Digital Misr Portal, and instant money transfers without requiring a traditional bank account. Massive Growth: The bank has experienced exceptional growth rates, with deposits increasing by 157.7% from EGP 73.5 billion in December 2020 to EGP 189.4 billion in September 2024, and the loan portfolio growing by 136.2% from EGP 34 billion to EGP 80.3 billion in the same period.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Agricultural Bank of Egypt?
faq.answer: Agricultural Bank of Egypt is classified as a specialized public sector bank (بنك متخصص من القطاع العام) under Egyptian banking regulations. Specifically, it is an agricultural development bank that operates as a joint stock company wholly owned by the Egyptian government. The bank is subject to the provisions of Law No. 88 of 2003 regarding the Central Bank, the banking system, and monetary policy. While it maintains its specialized focus on agricultural financing and rural development, ABE also operates as a full-service commercial bank offering comprehensive banking services to all segments of Egyptian society. This dual nature allows it to serve both its developmental mission in the agricultural sector and provide general banking services to urban and rural customers alike.
